Container.Name: Mediastarr
Container.Overview:
Automated missing-content and quality-upgrade search for Sonarr and Radarr. Supports multiple instances with custom names (HD, 4K, Anime...), SQLite search history, configurable cooldown and daily limits, Discord webhook notifications, German and English UI, and a first-run browser wizard — no config file editing required.
Optional: set MEDIASTARR_PASSWORD to require login for the dashboard and API. Browser write requests are CSRF-protected automatically when a password is set.
Independent project. Not affiliated with Huntarr.
Container.Description: Automated media search for Sonarr and Radarr. Multi-instance, Discord notifications, SQLite history, optional password protection, DE/EN.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the Mediastarr web interface.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent storage for config.json and the SQLite search history database. Required — without this the setup wizard restarts on every container restart.

Config.Name: Sonarr API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: pre-configure Sonarr to skip the setup wizard. Find in Sonarr → Settings → General → Security → API Key.

Config.Name: Sonarr URL (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: Sonarr URL. Only used when SONARR_API_KEY is also set. Use the IP or hostname reachable from this container, e.g. http://192.168.1.100:8989

Config.Name: Radarr API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: pre-configure Radarr to skip the setup wizard. Find in Radarr → Settings → General → Security → API Key.

Config.Name: Radarr URL (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: Radarr URL. Only used when RADARR_API_KEY is also set. Use the IP or hostname reachable from this container, e.g. http://192.168.1.100:7878

Config.Name: Dashboard Password (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: if set, the dashboard and API require login with this password. Browser write actions are CSRF-protected automatically. Recommended when the WebUI is reachable outside a trusted LAN.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone. Used for all timestamps in the UI and logs. Example: Europe/Berlin, America/New_York, UTC
